◆ MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_FACTOR

#define MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_FACTOR   20.0

MASS_MAPS: per-segment PLC entries → HEALPix maps (products-only)

Purpose Detect entries of mass elements into a past light-cone (PLC) between adjacent fragmentation outputs and accumulate counts on one HEALPix map per segment.

High-level flow (per segment) 1) Build candidate replications by radial overlap with the segment chi-span. 2) Optionally partition local Lagrangian tile into blocks and apply conservative culls (radial shell + angular bound) before touching particles. 3) Traverse particles for passing block/replication pairs; detect PLC entry with H = chi(z) − r sign change, interpolate entry position, gate by aperture, pixelize, and accumulate. 4) MPI-reduce per-rep counters and maps; write one FITS per segment.

Requirements and interplay with other modules

  • Requires: PLC, RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS, HAVE_CFITSIO (enforced below).
  • Uses SNAPSHOT structures when MASS_MAPS_FILTER_UNCOLLAPSED is enabled.
  • Displacements between segments: • If SCALE_DEPENDENT (or READ_PK_TABLE/MOD_GRAV_FR) is defined, derivatives are recomputed each segment via FFTs (fragment.c → compute_displacements). • Otherwise (scale-independent ΛCDM), per-particle displacements are rescaled between segments using k-independent growth factors (fast path, no FFTs).

Key runtime parameters (from parameter file)

  • PLCAperture [deg]: Angular radius of the PLC cone; <180 selects a spherical cap map.
  • PLC axis/center: Provided by PLC setup; used for pixelization basis and origin.

Compile-time directives (macros) and what they control Core feature flags

  • MASS_MAPS Enable mass-maps module (this file).
  • PLC Enable past-light-cone logic (required).
  • RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS Maintain prev/curr segment fields for interpolation (required).
  • HAVE_CFITSIO Enable FITS output (required).
  • SNAPSHOT Provide product fields used by optional filters.

Optional behavior

  • MASS_MAPS_FILTER_UNCOLLAPSED Include only entries with z_plc > z_acc (requires SNAPSHOT).
  • MASS_MAPS_MATCH_DIAG Extra diagnostics to reconcile entries with PLC halo catalog.
  • MASS_MAPS_FULLSKY_OUTPUT If nonzero, also write full-sky IMAGE HDU even when aperture<180 (default 0).

Lagrangian sub-volume culling (performance)

  • MASS_MAPS_BLOCKS[_X|_Y|_Z] Number of Lagrangian blocks per axis (>=1). Default: 8 per axis.
  • MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_FACTOR Variable AABB padding factor (dimensionless). Default: 20.0. Units and conversion: • Model is defined in Mpc/h: pad_h = FACTOR * (sigma8/0.8)^{1.5} / (1+z) • Internally we work in true Mpc (positions use InterPartDist in Mpc), so we convert pad_phys = pad_h / Hubble100. • For histogram/header convenience, we report both PAD_PHYS(Mpc) and PAD_PHYS(Mpc/h), and also PAD_CELLS = PAD_PHYS(Mpc)/InterPartDist(Mpc).
  • MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_WARN_FRAC Warn on last segment if |x(q,z)-q| exceeds pad for more than this fraction. Default: 0.05.

Threading and parallelism

  • MASS_MAPS_THREAD_ACCUM Use per-thread HEALPix accumulators and merge at the end to reduce atomics.
  • USE_FFT_THREADS Enable FFTW threading for FFT phases (independent of mass-maps loops).

Output and diagnostics

  • For partial-sky (aperture<180), a RING-ordered spherical cap with Ncap pixels is produced. If the computed Ncap is 0 (too small aperture for the chosen NSIDE), maps are disabled with a warning.
  • FITS headers include provenance and filter stats when enabled: FILTER='ZPLC>ZACC', ZF_CONS/ZF_EXCL/ZF_INCL/ZF_FEXCL, APERTURE, and map geometry.

◆ aabb_distance_bounds_to_point()

static void aabb_distance_bounds_to_point ( const double  minv[3],
const double  maxv[3],
const double  c[3],
double *  dmin_out,
double *  dmax_out 
)
inlinestatic

aabb_distance_bounds_to_point

Purpose Compute tight lower and upper bounds for the Euclidean distance from a point c to an axis-aligned bounding box (AABB) defined by per-axis minima and maxima.

Parameters

  • minv 3-vector of AABB minimum coordinates (x_min, y_min, z_min).
  • maxv 3-vector of AABB maximum coordinates (x_max, y_max, z_max).
  • c 3-vector of point coordinates.
  • dmin_out If non-NULL, receives the minimum possible distance from c to any point in the AABB (0 if c lies inside the box).
  • dmax_out If non-NULL, receives the maximum possible distance from c to any point in the AABB (distance to the farthest corner).

Behavior

  • d_min is computed as the length of the per-axis clamp residuals (zero when c is inside the interval [minv,maxv] on that axis).
  • d_max is computed as the distance to the farthest AABB corner from c, found by taking the larger absolute delta on each axis.

Invariants

  • For any inputs, d_min <= d_max, and both are >= 0.
  • Units follow the inputs (e.g., true Mpc when min/max are given in Mpc).

Notes

  • Used for quick culling against the PLC spherical shell: compare these bounds with the segment's chi interval to early-accept or early-reject replications before finer tests.

Definition at line 1460 of file write_mass_maps.c.

1461{
1462 /* d_min: point-to-box distance */
1463 double d2min = 0.0;
1464 for (int a = 0; a < 3; ++a)
1465 {
1466 double da = 0.0;
1467 if (c[a] < minv[a])
1468 da = minv[a] - c[a];
1469 else if (c[a] > maxv[a])
1470 da = c[a] - maxv[a];
1471 d2min += da * da;
1472 }
1473 /* d_max: farthest corner distance */
1474 double d2max = 0.0;
1475 for (int a = 0; a < 3; ++a)
1476 {
1477 double da0 = fabs(minv[a] - c[a]);
1478 double da1 = fabs(maxv[a] - c[a]);
1479 double da = (da0 > da1) ? da0 : da1;
1480 d2max += da * da;
1481 }
1482 if (dmin_out)
1483 *dmin_out = sqrt(d2min);
1484 if (dmax_out)
1485 *dmax_out = sqrt(d2max);
1486}

◆ mass_maps_accumulate_entry_pos()

static void mass_maps_accumulate_entry_pos ( int  s,
const double  entry_pos[3],
double  weight 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_accumulate_entry_pos

Purpose Accumulate a scalar weight into the HEALPix map for a given mass sheet segment at the pixel corresponding to an absolute entry position.

Parameters

  • s Segment index (0 .. NMassSheets-1).
  • entry_pos Absolute entry position in true Mpc (same frame as PLC).
  • weight Scalar to add to the corresponding pixel (e.g., 1.0).

Behavior

  • Validates segment and NSIDE; computes the pixel via mass_maps_compute_pixel_from_pos(entry_pos, MassMapNSIDE_current, &ipix).
  • If the segment map exists and ipix lies within [0, MassMapNPIX), adds 'weight' to map[ipix]. Out-of-range pixels are ignored.

Threading

  • This helper does not perform any synchronization. Callers must ensure either single-threaded use, per-thread private maps (later reduced), or protect the update with an atomic/lock at the call site when sharing a global map among threads.

◆ mass_maps_axis_pixel_ring()

static long mass_maps_axis_pixel_ring ( int  nside)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_axis_pixel_ring

Purpose Return the HEALPix pixel index (RING ordering) at polar angle theta=0 and phi=0 for a given NSIDE. In this module, theta=0 corresponds to the PLC axis direction used to orient maps.

Parameters

  • nside HEALPix NSIDE (power-of-two, >0 expected).

Returns Pixel index in [0, 12*nside^2 - 1] on success; -1 if the mapping fails or nside is invalid.

Notes

  • Uses RING ordering (ang2pix_ring). Not valid for NESTED ordering.
  • This is a convenience helper for diagnostics and header metadata.

◆ mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center()

static void mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center ( void  )
inlinestatic

mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center

Purpose Lazily cache the PLC center in physical units (true Mpc) and the orthonormal sky basis vectors (e1,e2,e3) used for pixelization and aperture tests. Avoids recomputing these per call in hot paths.

Behavior

  • If the cache is already initialized (MassMapPLC_BasisReady!=0), returns.
  • Otherwise, converts plc.center (grid units) to physical units via InterPartDist, and copies the precomputed unit basis vectors (plc.xvers, plc.yvers, plc.zvers) into local statics.

Notes

  • Units: positions scaled by params.InterPartDist (true Mpc).
  • Threading: benign double-init is possible if called concurrently, but values are idempotent and identical, so no functional impact.

◆ mass_maps_compute_pixel_from_pos()

int mass_maps_compute_pixel_from_pos ( const double  pos[3],
int  nside,
long *  pix_out 
)

mass_maps_compute_pixel_from_pos

Purpose Compute the HEALPix RING pixel index corresponding to an absolute 3D position expressed in physical units (true Mpc), using the PLC orientation (e1,e2,e3) and center previously cached.

Parameters

  • pos Absolute position (true Mpc) in the same frame as the cached PLC center.
  • nside HEALPix NSIDE (power-of-two, >0).
  • pix_out Output pointer; on success receives the pixel index (>=0).

Returns 1 on success; 0 on invalid inputs (pix_out==NULL or nside<=0), when the position coincides with the PLC center (undefined direction), or if the RING mapping fails.

Behavior

  • Translates pos by the cached PLC center and normalizes to obtain vhat.
  • Computes spherical angles relative to the PLC basis: cos(theta) = vhat·e3, phi = atan2(vhat·e2, vhat·e1) in [0,2π).
  • Calls ang2pix_ring(NSIDE, theta, phi) and stores the result in pix_out.

Notes

  • Uses RING ordering only. For partial-sky outputs, callers must also check that the returned pixel lies within the active prefix (0..Ncap-1).
  • Invokes mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center() to ensure the PLC center and basis are available.

Definition at line 1519 of file write_mass_maps.c.

1520{
1521 if (!pix_out || nside <= 0)
1522 return 0;
1523 /* Cache center/basis once per process to avoid recomputing in hot paths */
1524 mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center();
1525 double vx = pos[0] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[0];
1526 double vy = pos[1] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[1];
1527 double vz = pos[2] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[2];
1528 double vnorm = sqrt(vx * vx + vy * vy + vz * vz);
1529 if (vnorm == 0.0)
1530 return 0;
1531 double invv = 1.0 / vnorm;
1532 double vhat[3] = {vx * invv, vy * invv, vz * invv};
1533 const double *e1 = MassMapPLC_e1;
1534 const double *e2 = MassMapPLC_e2;
1535 const double *e3 = MassMapPLC_e3;
1536 double cos_theta = vhat[0] * e3[0] + vhat[1] * e3[1] + vhat[2] * e3[2];
1537 if (cos_theta > 1.0)
1538 cos_theta = 1.0;
1539 else if (cos_theta < -1.0)
1540 cos_theta = -1.0;
1541 double theta = acos(cos_theta);
1542 double x1 = vhat[0] * e1[0] + vhat[1] * e1[1] + vhat[2] * e1[2];
1543 double y1 = vhat[0] * e2[0] + vhat[1] * e2[1] + vhat[2] * e2[2];
1544 double phi = atan2(y1, x1);
1545 if (phi < 0.0)
1546 {
1547 double twopi = 2.0 * acos(-1.0);
1548 phi += twopi;
1549 }
1550 long ipix = -1;
1551 /* RING-only ordering */
1552 ang2pix_ring((long)nside, theta, phi, &ipix);
1553 if (ipix < 0)
1554 return 0;
1555 *pix_out = ipix;
1556 return 1;
1557}

◆ mass_maps_compute_prev_curr_positions()

static void mass_maps_compute_prev_curr_positions ( const pos_data *  p,
double  pos_prev[3],
double  pos_curr[3],
int  order 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_compute_prev_curr_positions

Purpose Build Eulerian positions for a particle at the previous and current segment endpoints from its stored LPT displacements, producing two absolute positions in physical units (true Mpc).

Parameters

  • p Pointer to pos_data with LPT displacement components.
  • pos_prev Output array [3]; receives position at previous endpoint.
  • pos_curr Output array [3]; receives position at current endpoint.
  • order LPT order to include: 1, 2 (requires TWO_LPT), or 3 (requires TWO_LPT and THREE_LPT). Higher terms are summed into both prev and curr as available.

Behavior

  • Forms endpoint displacements by selecting per-endpoint components: prev: q + (v_prev [+ v2_prev [+ v31_prev + v32_prev]]) curr: q + (v [+ v2 [+ v31 + v32 ]])
  • Scales both positions from lattice units (cells) to physical box units using params.InterPartDist (true Mpc).

Returns void. Outputs are written to pos_prev and pos_curr.

Notes

  • Requires RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS (enforced for MASS_MAPS at compile time).
  • Replication shifts are NOT applied here; callers add them per replication.
  • Periodic wrapping, if desired, is also the caller's responsibility.

Definition at line 1998 of file write_mass_maps.c.

2002{
2003 /* Sum displacements per order (endpoints: weights collapse to selecting
2004 either *_prev or current components, so no interpolation weights used). */
2005 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i)
2006 {
2007 double dp_prev = p->v_prev[i];
2008 double dp_curr = p->v[i];
2009#ifdef TWO_LPT
2010 if (order > 1)
2011 {
2012 dp_prev += p->v2_prev[i];
2013 dp_curr += p->v2[i];
2014 }
2015#ifdef THREE_LPT
2016 if (order > 2)
2017 {
2018 dp_prev += p->v31_prev[i] + p->v32_prev[i];
2019 dp_curr += p->v31[i] + p->v32[i];
2020 }
2021#endif /* THREE_LPT */
2022#endif /* TWO_LPT */
2023 pos_prev[i] = p->q[i] + dp_prev;
2024 pos_curr[i] = p->q[i] + dp_curr;
2025 }
2026 /* Rescale from lattice units (grid cells) to physical box units using InterPartDist. */
2027 double scale = params.InterPartDist; /* typical: BoxSize / GridSize */
2028 pos_prev[0] *= scale;
2029 pos_prev[1] *= scale;
2030 pos_prev[2] *= scale;
2031 pos_curr[0] *= scale;
2032 pos_curr[1] *= scale;
2033 pos_curr[2] *= scale;
2034}

◆ mass_maps_crossing_from_shifted_positions()

static int mass_maps_crossing_from_shifted_positions ( const double  Pprev[3],
const double  Pcurr[3],
const double  c_phys[3],
double  chi_prev,
double  chi_curr,
double *  alpha_out,
double  entry_pos[3] 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_crossing_from_shifted_positions

Purpose Detect and locate a radial crossing of the PLC spherical boundary along a particle segment, using absolute positions that already include the replication shift, and precomputed chi values at the segment endpoints. The boundary is defined by r(alpha) = chi(alpha), with H = chi - r, and linear interpolation in alpha between endpoints.

Parameters

  • Pprev Absolute position at the previous endpoint (true Mpc), replication shift already applied.
  • Pcurr Absolute position at the current endpoint (true Mpc), replication shift already applied.
  • c_phys PLC center in true Mpc (same frame/units as Pprev/Pcurr).
  • chi_prev Comoving distance chi at the previous endpoint (Mpc). In the present-epoch frame used here, this equals the corresponding physical distance, so it is directly comparable to r.
  • chi_curr Comoving distance chi at the current endpoint (Mpc).
  • alpha_out Optional; on success, receives the segment interpolation parameter alpha in [0,1] locating the crossing.
  • entry_pos Optional; on success, receives the 3D crossing position Pprev + alpha * (Pcurr - Pprev).

Returns 1 if a crossing is detected and the intersection is computed; otherwise 0.

Behavior

  • Let H_prev = chi_prev - |Pprev - c| and H_curr = chi_curr - |Pcurr - c|. A crossing is reported only for the inside→outside case (H_prev > 0 && H_curr <= 0).
  • Uses linear interpolation of H in alpha and solves H(alpha)=0 via alpha = H_prev / (H_prev - H_curr), with a small-denominator guard using MASS_MAPS_F_EPS. The resulting alpha is clamped to [0,1].

Notes

  • Purely local computation; no shared state is modified.
  • Caller must ensure units are consistent: P*, c_phys in true Mpc; chi_* in Mpc (comoving), which here are comparable to present-epoch physical Mpc.

Definition at line 1384 of file write_mass_maps.c.

1391{
1392 /* Distances to PLC center */
1393 double dx0 = Pprev[0] - c_phys[0];
1394 double dy0 = Pprev[1] - c_phys[1];
1395 double dz0 = Pprev[2] - c_phys[2];
1396 double dx1 = Pcurr[0] - c_phys[0];
1397 double dy1 = Pcurr[1] - c_phys[1];
1398 double dz1 = Pcurr[2] - c_phys[2];
1399 double r_prev = sqrt(dx0 * dx0 + dy0 * dy0 + dz0 * dz0);
1400 double r_curr = sqrt(dx1 * dx1 + dy1 * dy1 + dz1 * dz1);
1401 double H_prev = chi_prev - r_prev;
1402 double H_curr = chi_curr - r_curr;
1403 if (!(H_prev > 0.0 && H_curr <= 0.0))
1404 return 0;
1405 double denom = H_prev - H_curr;
1406 if (fabs(denom) < MASS_MAPS_F_EPS)
1407 return 0;
1408 double alpha = H_prev / denom;
1409 if (alpha < 0.0)
1410 alpha = 0.0;
1411 else if (alpha > 1.0)
1412 alpha = 1.0;
1413 if (alpha_out)
1414 *alpha_out = alpha;
1415 if (entry_pos)
1416 {
1417 entry_pos[0] = Pprev[0] + alpha * (Pcurr[0] - Pprev[0]);
1418 entry_pos[1] = Pprev[1] + alpha * (Pcurr[1] - Pprev[1]);
1419 entry_pos[2] = Pprev[2] + alpha * (Pcurr[2] - Pprev[2]);
1420 }
1421 return 1;
1422}

◆ mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint()

static void mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint ( int  use_curr,
unsigned long long *  hist_local,
unsigned long long *  underflow_local,
unsigned long long *  overflow_local,
unsigned long long *  sampled_local 
)
static

mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint

Purpose Accumulate a local displacement histogram for one endpoint selector: use_curr=0 for the previous endpoint ("prev"), use_curr=1 for the current endpoint ("curr"). Displacements are |x(q,z)-q| in InterPartDist units (i.e., multiples of the InterPartDist). Underflow/overflow and total sampled counters are tracked alongside per-bin counts.

Parameters

  • use_curr 0: use products.*_prev fields; 1: use current products.* fields.
  • hist_local Output array of length DISP_HIST_NBINS (must be zero-initialized by caller).
  • underflow_local Output: incremented by samples with d < DMIN.
  • overflow_local Output: incremented by samples with d >= DMAX.
  • sampled_local Output: incremented by total samples considered (after stride filter).

Behavior

  • Iterates over this task's local FFT tile; optional sub-sampling via DISP_HIST_STRIDE by applying a deterministic (i+j+k) % stride == 0 filter on global indices.
  • Builds endpoint displacement magnitude per lattice site including compiled LPT orders.
  • Uses per-thread private histograms and reduces into hist_local to minimize contention.

Threading OpenMP is supported; per-thread histograms are accumulated with a final reduction. No MPI calls happen here; caller performs cross-task reductions/writes.

Definition at line 258 of file write_mass_maps.c.

263{
264 const int nb = (int)DISP_HIST_NBINS;
265 const double dmin = (double)DISP_HIST_DMIN;
266 const double dmax = (double)DISP_HIST_DMAX;
267 const int stride = (int)DISP_HIST_STRIDE;
268 if (nb <= 0 || dmax <= dmin)
269 return;
270 int nx = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_x_];
271 int ny = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_y_];
272 int nz = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];

274 int gy0 = (int)MyGrids[0].GSstart[_y_];
275 int gz0 = (int)MyGrids[0].GSstart[_z_];
276 double invw = (double)nb / (dmax - dmin);
277
278 unsigned long long uf = 0ULL, of = 0ULL, tot = 0ULL;
279
280#ifdef _OPENMP
281 int nthreads = omp_get_max_threads();
282#else
283 int nthreads = 1;
284#endif
285 unsigned long long *thread_hist = (unsigned long long *)calloc((size_t)nthreads * (size_t)nb, sizeof(unsigned long long));
286 if (!thread_hist)
287 return;
288
289 /* Parallel over local tile */
290#ifdef _OPENMP
291#pragma omp parallel
292#endif
293 {
294#ifdef _OPENMP
295 int tid = omp_get_thread_num();
296#else
297 int tid = 0;
298#endif
299 unsigned long long *h = thread_hist + (size_t)tid * (size_t)nb;
300
301#ifdef _OPENMP
302#pragma omp for collapse(3) schedule(static) reduction(+ : uf, of, tot)
303#endif
304 for (int li = 0; li < nx; ++li)
305 for (int lj = 0; lj < ny; ++lj)
306 for (int lk = 0; lk < nz; ++lk)
307 {
308 if (stride > 1)
309 {
310 int gi = gx0 + li, gj = gy0 + lj, gk = gz0 + lk;
311 /* Simple deterministic sub-sampling */
312 if (((gi + gj + gk) % stride) != 0)
313 continue;
314 }
315 unsigned int idx = COORD_TO_INDEX(li, lj, lk, MyGrids[0].GSlocal);

317 double vx = 0.0, vy = 0.0, vz = 0.0;
318 if (use_curr)
319 {
320 vx = products[idx].Vel[0];
321 vy = products[idx].Vel[1];
322 vz = products[idx].Vel[2];
323#ifdef TWO_LPT
324 vx += products[idx].Vel_2LPT[0];
325 vy += products[idx].Vel_2LPT[1];
326 vz += products[idx].Vel_2LPT[2];
327#ifdef THREE_LPT
328 vx += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1[0] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2[0];
329 vy += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1[1] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2[1];
330 vz += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1[2] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2[2];
331#endif
332#endif
333 }
334 else
335 {
336 vx = products[idx].Vel_prev[0];
337 vy = products[idx].Vel_prev[1];
338 vz = products[idx].Vel_prev[2];
339#ifdef TWO_LPT
340 vx += products[idx].Vel_2LPT_prev[0];
341 vy += products[idx].Vel_2LPT_prev[1];
342 vz += products[idx].Vel_2LPT_prev[2];
343#ifdef THREE_LPT
344 vx += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[0] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[0];
345 vy += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[1] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[1];
346 vz += products[idx].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[2] + products[idx].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[2];
347#endif
348#endif
349 }
350 double d = sqrt(vx * vx + vy * vy + vz * vz); /* in InterPartDist units */
351 ++tot;
352 if (d < dmin)
353 {
354 ++uf;
355 continue;
356 }
357 if (!(d < dmax))
358 {
359 ++of;
360 continue;
361 }
362 int bin = (int)floor((d - dmin) * invw);
363 if (bin < 0)
364 bin = 0;
365 if (bin >= nb)
366 bin = nb - 1;
367 h[bin]++;
368 }
369 }
370
371 /* Reduce thread hist to task hist */
372 for (int t = 0; t < nthreads; ++t)
373 {
374 unsigned long long *h = thread_hist + (size_t)t * (size_t)nb;
375 for (int b = 0; b < nb; ++b)
376 hist_local[b] += h[b];
377 }
378 free(thread_hist);

380 *overflow_local += of;
381 *sampled_local += tot;
382}

◆ mass_maps_disp_hist_on_segment()

static void mass_maps_disp_hist_on_segment ( int  segment_index,
double  z_prev,
double  z_curr 
)
static

mass_maps_disp_hist_on_segment

Purpose Orchestrate per-segment displacement histograms at the two segment endpoints (previous and current). Optionally computes for each endpoint depending on DISP_HIST_AT_PREV / DISP_HIST_AT_CURR, performs MPI reductions to rank 0, and writes ASCII histogram files via mass_maps_disp_hist_write_file.

Parameters

  • segment_index Index of the current fragmentation segment (not used for I/O; retained for context and future use).
  • z_prev Redshift at previous endpoint of the segment.
  • z_curr Redshift at current endpoint of the segment.

Behavior

  • Allocates per-endpoint task-local histograms (length DISP_HIST_NBINS) when enabled.
  • Invokes mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint for prev/curr to fill local bins and counters (underflow/overflow/sampled) in InterPartDist units.
  • Reduces bins and counters across MPI tasks to rank 0 and writes one file per enabled endpoint using z_prev/z_curr in the filename and header.
  • Frees temporary buffers.

Notes

  • Units: The accumulator operates in InterPartDist (true Mpc) units; the writer prints both InterPartDist and variable pad context in headers. No unit conversion happens here.
  • Concurrency: This function uses MPI_Reduce but no OpenMP loops directly.

Definition at line 412 of file write_mass_maps.c.

414 (void)segment_index;
415 const int nb = (int)DISP_HIST_NBINS;
416 if (nb <= 0 || DISP_HIST_DMAX <= DISP_HIST_DMIN)
417 return;
418
419 /* Allocate task-local buffers (conditional on compile-time toggles) */
420 unsigned long long *hist_prev_local = NULL, *hist_curr_local = NULL;
421 unsigned long long uf_prev_local = 0ULL, of_prev_local = 0ULL, tot_prev_local = 0ULL;
422 unsigned long long uf_curr_local = 0ULL, of_curr_local = 0ULL, tot_curr_local = 0ULL;
423
424 if (DISP_HIST_AT_PREV)
425 hist_prev_local = (unsigned long long *)calloc((size_t)nb, sizeof(unsigned long long));
426 if (DISP_HIST_AT_CURR)
427 hist_curr_local = (unsigned long long *)calloc((size_t)nb, sizeof(unsigned long long));
428
429 if (hist_prev_local)
430 mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint(0, hist_prev_local, &uf_prev_local, &of_prev_local, &tot_prev_local);
431 if (hist_curr_local)
432 mass_maps_disp_hist_accumulate_endpoint(1, hist_curr_local, &uf_curr_local, &of_curr_local, &tot_curr_local);
433
434 /* Global reductions (MPI) and writes on rank 0 */
435 if (hist_prev_local)
436 {
437 unsigned long long *hist_prev_global = NULL;
438 if (!ThisTask)
439 hist_prev_global = (unsigned long long *)calloc((size_t)nb, sizeof(unsigned long long));
440 MPI_Reduce(hist_prev_local, (!ThisTask ? hist_prev_global : NULL), nb, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
441 unsigned long long uf_prev_global = 0ULL, of_prev_global = 0ULL, tot_prev_global = 0ULL;
442 MPI_Reduce(&uf_prev_local, &uf_prev_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
443 MPI_Reduce(&of_prev_local, &of_prev_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
444 MPI_Reduce(&tot_prev_local, &tot_prev_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
445 mass_maps_disp_hist_write_file(z_prev, hist_prev_global, uf_prev_global, of_prev_global, tot_prev_global);
446 if (!ThisTask && hist_prev_global)
447 free(hist_prev_global);
448 }
449 if (hist_curr_local)
450 {
451 unsigned long long *hist_curr_global = NULL;
452 if (!ThisTask)
453 hist_curr_global = (unsigned long long *)calloc((size_t)nb, sizeof(unsigned long long));
454 MPI_Reduce(hist_curr_local, (!ThisTask ? hist_curr_global : NULL), nb, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
455 unsigned long long uf_curr_global = 0ULL, of_curr_global = 0ULL, tot_curr_global = 0ULL;
456 MPI_Reduce(&uf_curr_local, &uf_curr_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
457 MPI_Reduce(&of_curr_local, &of_curr_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
458 MPI_Reduce(&tot_curr_local, &tot_curr_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
459 mass_maps_disp_hist_write_file(z_curr, hist_curr_global, uf_curr_global, of_curr_global, tot_curr_global);
460 if (!ThisTask && hist_curr_global)
461 free(hist_curr_global);
462 }
463 if (hist_prev_local)
464 free(hist_prev_local);
465 if (hist_curr_local)

467}

static void mass_maps_disp_hist_write_file ( double  z_target,

)
static

Write an ASCII histogram file on rank 0

mass_maps_disp_hist_write_file

Purpose Write the global displacement histogram |x(q,z)-q| (measured in InterPartDist units) to an ASCII file. Only MPI rank 0 performs I/O.

Inputs

  • z_target Redshift at which the endpoint histogram was sampled.
  • hist_global Array of length DISP_HIST_NBINS with global bin counts (already MPI-reduced across tasks).
  • underflow_global Global count of samples with d < DMIN.
  • overflow_global Global count of samples with d >= DMAX.
  • sampled_global Global number of samples considered (including under/overflow).

Behavior and file format

  • Filename: pinocchio.<z>.<RunFlag>.disp_hist.out where <z> is formatted with 4 decimals for stable sorting/comparisons.
  • Header: prints grid/analysis provenance and the variable AABB pad used by mass-maps at this redshift, reported in Mpc, Mpc/h, and PAD_CELLS (PAD_PHYS / InterPartDist) for diagnostics.
  • Data rows (one per bin): bin_lo bin_hi count fraction cum_fraction

Units Histogram d values are in InterPartDist units.

Thread-safety / MPI Inputs must be globally reduced; only rank 0 writes.

Definition at line 154 of file write_mass_maps.c.

159{
160 /* Only root performs I/O; other ranks return immediately */
161 if (ThisTask != 0)
162 return;
163 /* Build filename: pinocchio.<z>.<RunFlag>.disp_hist.out with fixed-width z */
164 char fname[4 * LBLENGTH];
165 snprintf(fname, sizeof(fname), "pinocchio.%.4f.%s.disp_hist.out", z_target, params.RunFlag);
166 FILE *fd = fopen(fname, "w");
167 if (!fd)
168 {
169 fprintf(stderr, "Task 0 ERROR: cannot open %s for writing.\n", fname);
170 return;
171 }
172 double dmin = (double)DISP_HIST_DMIN;
173 double dmax = (double)DISP_HIST_DMAX;
174 int nb = (int)DISP_HIST_NBINS;
175 int stride = (int)DISP_HIST_STRIDE;
176 /* Compute variable buffer pad at this redshift for header context */
177 double sigma8 = params.Sigma8;
178 double factor = MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_FACTOR;
179 double res = params.InterPartDist; /* true Mpc */
180 double h = params.Hubble100;
181 double pad_phys_h = factor * pow(sigma8 / 0.8, 1.5) / (1.0 + z_target); /* Mpc/h */
182 double pad_phys = (h > 0.0) ? (pad_phys_h / h) : pad_phys_h; /* true Mpc */
183 double pad_cells = (res > 0.0) ? (pad_phys / res) : 0.0; /* dimensionless */
184 /* Header with provenance and units */
185 fprintf(fd, "# Displacement histogram |x(q,z)-q| in InterPartDist units\n");
186 fprintf(fd, "# z=%.8f NBINS=%d DMIN=%.6g DMAX=%.6g SPACING=linear STRIDE=%d\n", z_target, nb, dmin, dmax, stride);
187 fprintf(fd, "# GRID=(%d,%d,%d) InterPartDist(Mpc)=%.9g LPT_ORDER=%d MPI_TASKS=%d RUN=%s\n",
188 (int)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_], (int)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_], (int)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_],
189 params.InterPartDist,
190#ifdef THREE_LPT
191 3,
192#else
193#ifdef TWO_LPT
194 2,
195#else
196 1,
197#endif
198#endif
199 NTasks, params.RunFlag);
200 fprintf(fd, "# VARPAD: FACTOR=%.6g SIGMA8=%.6g PAD_PHYS(Mpc)=%.9g PAD_PHYS(Mpc/h)=%.9g PAD_CELLS=%.9g\n",
201 factor, sigma8, pad_phys, pad_phys_h, pad_cells);
202 fprintf(fd, "# SAMPLED_GLOBAL=%llu UNDERFLOW=%llu OVERFLOW=%llu\n",
203 (unsigned long long)sampled_global,
204 (unsigned long long)underflow_global,
205 (unsigned long long)overflow_global);
206 fprintf(fd, "# Columns: bin_lo bin_hi count fraction cum_fraction\n");
207 /* Emit rows */
208 double width = (nb > 0) ? ((dmax - dmin) / (double)nb) : 0.0;
209 unsigned long long cum = 0ULL;
210 for (int b = 0; b < nb; ++b)
211 {
212 double blo = dmin + width * (double)b;
213 double bhi = blo + width;
214 unsigned long long c = hist_global[b];
215 cum += c;
216 double frac = (sampled_global > 0ULL) ? ((double)c / (double)sampled_global) : 0.0;
217 double cfrac = (sampled_global > 0ULL) ? ((double)cum / (double)sampled_global) : 0.0;
218 fprintf(fd, "%.9g %.9g %llu %.9g %.9g\n", blo, bhi, (unsigned long long)c, frac, cfrac);
219 }
220 fclose(fd);
221 if (internal.verbose_level >= VDIAG)
222 {
223 printf("[%s] DISP_HIST: wrote %s (z=%.6f nbins=%d sampled=%llu uf=%llu of=%llu)\n",
224 fdate(), fname, z_target, nb,
225 (unsigned long long)sampled_global,
226 (unsigned long long)underflow_global,
227 (unsigned long long)overflow_global);
228 }
229}

◆ mass_maps_entry_inside_aperture()

static int mass_maps_entry_inside_aperture ( const double  pos[3])
inlinestatic

mass_maps_entry_inside_aperture

Purpose Test whether an absolute position (physical units) lies inside the PLC angular aperture (spherical cap) centered on the PLC axis.

Parameters

  • pos Absolute position in true Mpc (same units as InterPartDist-scaled positions elsewhere in mass-maps).

Returns 1 if the angle between (pos - PLC_center) and the PLC axis (plc.zvers) is <= params.PLCAperture [deg]; otherwise 0. Returns 0 for the degenerate case where the vector from center has zero length.

Notes

  • Uses the cached center/basis via mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center().
  • Clamps numerical round-off on cosines to [-1,1] before acos.

Definition at line 794 of file write_mass_maps.c.

795{
796 mass_maps_cache_plc_basis_and_center();
797 double vx = pos[0] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[0];
798 double vy = pos[1] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[1];
799 double vz = pos[2] - MassMapPLC_CenterPhys[2];
800 double vnorm = sqrt(vx * vx + vy * vy + vz * vz);
801 if (vnorm == 0.0)
802 return 0;
803 double invv = 1.0 / vnorm;
804 double vhat_z = (vx * MassMapPLC_e3[0] + vy * MassMapPLC_e3[1] + vz * MassMapPLC_e3[2]) * invv;
805 if (vhat_z > 1.0)
806 vhat_z = 1.0;
807 else if (vhat_z < -1.0)
808 vhat_z = -1.0;
809 double angle = acos(vhat_z) * (180.0 / acos(-1.0));
810 double A = params.PLCAperture;
811 return angle <= A;
812}

◆ mass_maps_free_maps()

void mass_maps_free_maps ( void  )

mass_maps_free_maps

Purpose Release HEALPix map memory owned by this module and reset associated state so maps are considered uninitialized.

Behavior

  • Frees MassMapSegmentMaps (per-segment map buffer) if allocated and sets the pointer to NULL.
  • Frees MassMapReduceBuffer (temporary MPI reduction buffer) if allocated and sets the pointer to NULL.
  • Resets MassMapNSIDE_current and MassMapNPIX to 0 to mark maps as absent.

Notes

  • Idempotent: safe to call multiple times.
  • Does not modify MassMapNCAP; callers that change NSIDE/aperture should recompute NCAP during re-initialization (via mass_maps_init_healpix_maps).

◆ mass_maps_get_group_id_global()

static int mass_maps_get_group_id_global ( int  ig,
int  jg,
int  kg 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_get_group_id_global

Purpose Return the group_ID assigned to the particle at GLOBAL lattice coordinates (ig, jg, kg) on this task's FFT tile. If the coordinates are outside this tile, returns 0.

Parameters

  • ig, jg, kg Global lattice indices of the particle.

Returns Integer group_ID for the particle (0 if none or if out of range).

Behavior

  • Translates global to local indices using MyGrids[0]. If any index is outside the local tile bounds, returns 0.
  • Otherwise, loads products[idx].group_ID and returns it.

Notes

  • Available only when SNAPSHOT is defined.
  • Caller must ensure that the queried (ig,jg,kg) lies on this task's tile when a non-zero ID is expected; no MPI communication occurs here.

2210 return 0;

static double mass_maps_get_zacc_global ( int  ig,
int  jg,
int  kg 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_get_zacc_global

Purpose Access the per-particle collapse redshift (ZACC) from the products array using GLOBAL lattice coordinates (ig, jg, kg) on this task's FFT tile.

Parameters

  • ig, jg, kg Global lattice indices of the particle.

Returns The collapse redshift zacc as a double if the coordinates map inside this task's local tile; otherwise returns a large negative sentinel (-1e30) indicating "never collapsed / out of range".

Notes

  • No MPI: caller must ensure the queried site resides on this rank if a valid value is expected.
  • Available only when SNAPSHOT is defined.

◆ mass_maps_init_healpix_maps()

int mass_maps_init_healpix_maps ( int  nside)

mass_maps_init_healpix_maps

Purpose Ensure per-segment HEALPix maps are allocated and initialized for the requested NSIDE, honoring the configured PLC aperture and output mode.

Parameters

  • nside Desired HEALPix NSIDE (power-of-two, >0).

Behavior

  • Frees any existing maps, then computes: npix_full = 12 * nside^2 Ncap = mass_maps_ncap_from_aperture(nside, params.PLCAperture) and selects MassMapNPIX based on MASS_MAPS_FULLSKY_OUTPUT: • FULLSKY: NPIX = npix_full (entire sphere) • CAP: NPIX = Ncap (RING prefix 0..Ncap-1 around north pole)
  • Allocates a flat buffer of doubles of size NMassSheets * NPIX and zeros it.
  • On rank 0 and at diagnostic verbosity, prints a summary (ORDERING=RING).

Returns 0 on success; 1 if inputs are invalid, memory allocation fails, or the computed NPIX is zero (maps disabled). In failure cases, internal state (MassMapNSIDE_current, MassMapNPIX, MassMapNCAP) is reset to 0.

Notes

  • The map memory is owned by this module and must be released with mass_maps_free_maps().
  • Aperture is in degrees; cap computation is analytic in RING geometry.

Definition at line 1201 of file write_mass_maps.c.

◆ mass_maps_init_sheets()

int mass_maps_init_sheets ( void  )

mass_maps_init_sheets

Build the array of MassSheet structures from the global output redshift list. Validates:

  • At least 2 output redshifts.
  • Strictly descending ordering (z[i] > z[i+1]).
  • First / last redshifts match PLC StartingzForPLC / LastzForPLC.
  • No adjacent duplicates within MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS. Allocates:
  • MassSheets (NMassSheets = outputs.n - 1)
  • Boundary arrays (Z, Chi, DA) of length outputs.n. Precomputes per-sheet: chi / da bounds, deltas, inverse delta, chi^3 difference. Returns 0 on success, 1 on validation / allocation failure (after emitting message on task 0).

Definition at line 829 of file write_mass_maps.c.

830{
831 /* Preconditions: outputs.z[] filled, descending order, outputs.n >= 2 */
832 if (outputs.n < 2)
833 {
834 if (!ThisTask)
835 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Need at least 2 output redshifts (found %d) to define mass sheets.\n", outputs.n);
836 return 1;
837 }
838
839 /* Ensure coverage matches PLC redshift range: first output == StartingzForPLC, last output == LastzForPLC */
840 {
841 double z_first = outputs.z[0];
842 double z_last = outputs.z[outputs.n - 1];
843 if (fabs(z_first - params.StartingzForPLC) > MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS)
844 {
845 if (!ThisTask)
846 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: First output redshift %g does not match StartingzForPLC=%g (|Δ|=%g > %g).\n",
847 z_first, params.StartingzForPLC, fabs(z_first - params.StartingzForPLC), MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS);
848 return 1;
849 }
850 if (fabs(z_last - params.LastzForPLC) > MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS)
851 {
852 if (!ThisTask)
853 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Last output redshift %g does not match LastzForPLC=%g (|Δ|=%g > %g).\n",
854 z_last, params.LastzForPLC, fabs(z_last - params.LastzForPLC), MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS);
855 return 1;
856 }
857 }
858
859 /* Verify strictly descending and no duplicates within eps */
860 for (int i = 0; i < outputs.n - 1; ++i)
861 {
862 double z_hi = outputs.z[i];
863 double z_lo = outputs.z[i + 1];
864 if (!(z_hi > z_lo))
865 {
866 if (!ThisTask)
867 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Output redshifts must be in strictly descending order (z[%d]=%g, z[%d]=%g).\n", i, z_hi, i + 1, z_lo);
868 return 1;
869 }
870 if (fabs(z_hi - z_lo) < MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS)
871 {
872 if (!ThisTask)
873 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Adjacent output redshifts too close (|%g-%g| < %g).\n", z_hi, z_lo, MASS_MAPS_Z_EPS);
874 return 1;
875 }
876 }
877 /*
878 * Number of mass sheets equals the number of adjacent redshift pairs
879 * in the output list: sheets s = 0..(n-2) are bounded by z[s] and z[s+1].
880 * We therefore allocate:
881 * - MassSheets: one entry per sheet (outputs.n - 1)
882 * - Boundary arrays (Z/Chi/DA): one value per boundary, length outputs.n
883 * (there are outputs.n boundaries for outputs.n-1 sheets).
884 */
885 NMassSheets = outputs.n - 1;
886 MassSheets = (MassSheet *)malloc(sizeof(MassSheet) * NMassSheets);
887 MassMapBoundaryZ = (double *)malloc(sizeof(double) * outputs.n);
888 MassMapBoundaryChi = (double *)malloc(sizeof(double) * outputs.n);
889 MassMapBoundaryDA = (double *)malloc(sizeof(double) * outputs.n);
890 if (!MassSheets)
891 {
892 if (!ThisTask)
893 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Cannot allocate MassSheets (%d entries).\n", NMassSheets);
894 return 1;
895 }
896 if (!MassMapBoundaryZ || !MassMapBoundaryChi || !MassMapBoundaryDA)
897 {
898 if (!ThisTask)
899 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Cannot allocate boundary arrays (n=%d).\n", outputs.n);
900 return 1;
901 }
902
903 /* Fill boundary arrays */
904 for (int k = 0; k < outputs.n; ++k)
905 {
906 double z = outputs.z[k];
907 MassMapBoundaryZ[k] = z;
908 MassMapBoundaryChi[k] = ComovingDistance(z);
909 MassMapBoundaryDA[k] = DiameterDistance(z);
910 }
911
912 /*
913 * For each mass sheet s (bounded by output boundaries s and s+1),
914 * cache the upper/lower redshift (z), comoving distance (chi), and
915 * angular-diameter distance (da). Compute the spans Δz and Δχ, check
916 * that Δχ>0 (monotonic chi with descending z), store its inverse, and
917 * precompute χ_hi^3 − χ_lo^3 (useful for volume-weighted quantities).
918 */
919 for (int s = 0; s < NMassSheets; ++s)
920 {
921 MassSheet *ms = &MassSheets[s];
922 ms->z_hi = MassMapBoundaryZ[s];
923 ms->z_lo = MassMapBoundaryZ[s + 1];
924 ms->delta_z = ms->z_hi - ms->z_lo;
925 ms->chi_hi = MassMapBoundaryChi[s];
926 ms->chi_lo = MassMapBoundaryChi[s + 1];
927 ms->delta_chi = ms->chi_hi - ms->chi_lo;
928 if (!(ms->delta_chi > 0.0))
929 {
930 if (!ThisTask)
931 fprintf(stderr, "MASS_MAPS ERROR: Non-positive comoving distance span for sheet %d (chi_hi=%g chi_lo=%g).\n", s, ms->chi_hi, ms->chi_lo);
932 return 1;
933 }
934 ms->inv_dchi = 1.0 / ms->delta_chi;
935 ms->da_hi = MassMapBoundaryDA[s];
936 ms->da_lo = MassMapBoundaryDA[s + 1];
937 double chi_hi3 = ms->chi_hi * ms->chi_hi * ms->chi_hi;
938 double chi_lo3 = ms->chi_lo * ms->chi_lo * ms->chi_lo;
939 ms->chi3_diff = chi_hi3 - chi_lo3;
940 }
941
942 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDIAG)
943 {
944 printf("[%s] MASS_MAPS: initialized %d mass sheets\n", fdate(), NMassSheets);
945 for (int s = 0; s < NMassSheets; ++s)
946 {

◆ mass_maps_map_stats()

static void mass_maps_map_stats ( const double *  map,
long  npix,
double *  sum_out,
double *  min_out,
double *  max_out,
long *  nnz_out 
)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_map_stats

Purpose Compute simple statistics over a map buffer: sum, minimum, maximum, and the count of non-zero entries (nnz).

Parameters

  • map Pointer to the map array (length npix). Must be non-NULL if npix > 0. Not dereferenced when npix <= 0.
  • npix Number of pixels/elements in the map (may be 0).
  • sum_out If non-NULL, receives the sum of all elements (0.0 if npix==0).
  • min_out If non-NULL, receives the minimum value (0.0 if npix==0).
  • max_out If non-NULL, receives the maximum value (0.0 if npix==0).
  • nnz_out If non-NULL, receives the count of elements with value != 0.0.

Notes

  • Non-zero detection uses exact floating-point comparison (v != 0.0), with no epsilon tolerance. Callers needing a threshold should apply it externally.
  • When npix <= 0, outputs (if requested) are set to their neutral defaults: sum=0, min=0, max=0, nnz=0.

Definition at line 1285 of file write_mass_maps.c.

1287{
1288 double s = 0.0;
1289 double mn = 0.0;
1290 double mx = 0.0;
1291 long nnz = 0;
1292 if (npix > 0)
1293 {
1294 mn = mx = map[0];
1295 for (long i = 0; i < npix; ++i)
1296 {
1297 double v = map[i];
1298 s += v;
1299 if (v < mn)
1300 mn = v;
1301 if (v > mx)
1302 mx = v;
1303 if (v != 0.0)
1304 nnz++;
1305 }
1306 }
1307 if (sum_out)
1308 *sum_out = s;
1309 if (min_out)
1310 *min_out = mn;
1311 if (max_out)
1312 *max_out = mx;
1313 if (nnz_out)
1314 *nnz_out = nnz;
1315}

◆ mass_maps_ncap_from_aperture()

static long mass_maps_ncap_from_aperture ( int  nside,
double  aperture_deg 
)
static

mass_maps_ncap_from_aperture

Purpose Compute the number of HEALPix RING pixels contained in a spherical cap of angular radius 'aperture_deg' centered at theta=0 (i.e., around the north pole aligned with the PLC axis). The result corresponds to the contiguous prefix [0 .. Ncap-1] in RING ordering for a cap centered at the pole.

Parameters

  • nside HEALPix NSIDE (power-of-two, >0).
  • aperture_deg Spherical cap radius in degrees; clamped to [0, 180].

Returns The number of pixels Ncap in the cap (0 <= Ncap <= 12*Nside^2). For aperture=0, returns 0. For aperture>=180, returns full-sky count (12*Nside^2).

Notes

  • Uses analytic HEALPix ring geometry without calling chealpix: • North polar rings i=1..N-1: z_i = 1 - i^2/(3N^2), npix_i = 4i • Equatorial rings i=N..3N: z_i = (4N - 2i)/(3N), npix_i = 4N • South polar rings i=3N+1..4N-1 with m=4N-i: z_i = -1 + m^2/(3N^2), npix_i = 4m Rings with z >= z0, where z0=cos(theta_cap), are fully included.
  • O(1) cost; avoids per-pixel work and chealpix dependency.
  • The cap is symmetric about the pole, so the included pixels form a RING prefix.

Definition at line 1025 of file write_mass_maps.c.

1026{
1027 if (nside <= 0)
1028 return 0;
1029 long npix_full = mass_maps_npix_from_nside(nside);
1030 /* Clamp aperture to [0,180] */
1031 if (aperture_deg <= 0.0)
1032 return 0;
1033 if (aperture_deg >= 180.0)
1034 return npix_full;
1035 double pi = acos(-1.0);
1036 double theta_cap = aperture_deg * (pi / 180.0);
1037 double z0 = cos(theta_cap); /* include rings with z >= z0 */
1038
1039 long ncap = 0;
1040 int N = nside;
1041
1042 /* North polar rings: i = 1..N-1, z_i = 1 - i^2/(3 N^2), npix_i = 4 i */
1043 double tmp = 3.0 * (double)N * (double)N * (1.0 - z0);
1044 long ipolar_max = (long)floor(sqrt(fmax(0.0, tmp)) + 1e-12);
1045 if (ipolar_max > (long)N - 1)
1046 ipolar_max = (long)N - 1;
1047 if (ipolar_max > 0)
1048 {
1049 /* sum_{i=1}^{ipolar_max} 4 i = 2 * ipolar_max * (ipolar_max + 1) */
1050 ncap += 2L * ipolar_max * (ipolar_max + 1L);
1051 }
1052
1053 /* Equatorial rings: i = N..3N, z_i = (4N - 2i)/(3N), npix_i = 4N */
1054 double i2max_d = ((4.0 * N) - 3.0 * N * z0) / 2.0;
1055 long i2_max = (long)floor(i2max_d + 1e-12);
1056 if (i2_max > 3L * N)
1057 i2_max = 3L * N;
1058 if (i2_max >= (long)N)
1059 {
1060 long neq_rings = i2_max - (long)N + 1L;
1061 if (neq_rings > 0)
1062 ncap += (long)(4L * (long)N) * neq_rings;
1063 }
1064
1065 /* South polar rings: i = 3N+1..4N-1, z_i = -1 + m^2/(3N^2), where m = 4N - i, npix_i = 4 m */
1066 if (i2_max >= 3L * N)
1067 {
1068 /* Only if cap extends beyond equatorial band */
1069 double tmp_s = 3.0 * (double)N * (double)N * (1.0 + z0);
1070 long m_min = (long)ceil(sqrt(fmax(0.0, tmp_s)) - 1e-12);
1071 if (m_min < 1)
1072 m_min = 1;
1073 if (m_min <= (long)N - 1)
1074 {
1075 long m_max = (long)N - 1;
1076 long count = m_max - m_min + 1L;
1077 /* sum_{m=m_min}^{m_max} 4 m = 4 * ( (m_min + m_max) * count / 2 ) */
1078 long sum_m = (m_min + m_max) * count;
1079 ncap += 2L * sum_m;
1080 }
1081 }
1082 if (ncap > npix_full)
1083 ncap = npix_full;
1084 return ncap;
1085}

static long mass_maps_npix_from_nside ( int  nside)
inlinestatic

mass_maps_npix_from_nside

Purpose Return the total number of HEALPix pixels for a given NSIDE without requiring any external HEALPix library calls.

Parameters

  • nside HEALPix NSIDE (power-of-two, >0 expected).

Returns 12 * nside^2 as a long integer. For non-positive nside, the arithmetic still evaluates consistently to 0 or negative; callers are expected to validate nside beforehand.

◆ mass_maps_particle_sign_change()

int mass_maps_particle_sign_change ( int  rep_id,
const double  q[3],
const double  disp_prev[3],
const double  disp_curr[3],
double  z_prev,
double  z_curr,
double *  alpha_out,
double  entry_pos[3] 
)

mass_maps_particle_sign_change

Test whether a particle leaves the PLC cone during the latest segment for a specific replication, using H(z, r) = chi(z) - r with chi the comoving distance to redshift and r the distance to the PLC center. A crossing is detected when H_prev > 0 and H_curr <= 0 (inside -> outside across the segment).

Inputs: rep_id - replication index q[3] - base (Lagrangian) particle position disp_prev - previous total Eulerian displacement vector disp_curr - current total Eulerian displacement vector z_prev - previous redshift (used to compute chi_prev) z_curr - current redshift (used to compute chi_curr) alpha_out - (optional) on success, fraction in [0,1] from previous to current state entry_pos - (optional) interpolated Eulerian position at crossing

Notes:

  • If q == (0,0,0), disp_* are interpreted as absolute Eulerian positions. Otherwise positions are q + disp_*.
  • Replication shift equals (i,j,k) times the global box size in each axis.

Logic:

  1. Build previous and current replicated positions.
  2. Compute r_prev, r_curr and chi_prev=ComovingDistance(z_prev), chi_curr.
  3. H_prev = chi_prev - r_prev, H_curr = chi_curr - r_curr.
  4. Crossing when H_prev > 0 and H_curr <= 0.
  5. Interpolate alpha = H_prev / (H_prev - H_curr); clamp to [0,1]. Skip ambiguous cases where |H_prev - H_curr| < MASS_MAPS_F_EPS.

Returns 1 if a crossing is detected per the above condition, else 0.

Definition at line 2250 of file write_mass_maps.c.

2258{
2259 if (rep_id < 0 || rep_id >= plc.Nreplications)
2260 return 0;
2261 int ii = plc.repls[rep_id].i;
2262 int jj = plc.repls[rep_id].j;
2263 int kk = plc.repls[rep_id].k;
2264 /* Replication shift in physical units: integer global box counts only (no subbox offsets) */
2265 double Bx = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] * params.InterPartDist;
2266 double By = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] * params.InterPartDist;
2267 double Bz = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_] * params.InterPartDist;
2268 double shift[3] = {ii * Bx, jj * By, kk * Bz};
2269
2270 /* If q==0 vector, treat disp_* as absolute positions (already q+dp). Otherwise treat as displacements relative to q. */
2271 int q_is_zero = (q[0] == 0.0 && q[1] == 0.0 && q[2] == 0.0);
2272 double pos_prev[3], pos_curr[3];
2273 if (q_is_zero)
2274 {
2275 pos_prev[0] = disp_prev[0] + shift[0];
2276 pos_prev[1] = disp_prev[1] + shift[1];
2277 pos_prev[2] = disp_prev[2] + shift[2];
2278 pos_curr[0] = disp_curr[0] + shift[0];
2279 pos_curr[1] = disp_curr[1] + shift[1];
2280 pos_curr[2] = disp_curr[2] + shift[2];
2281 }
2282 else
2283 {
2284 pos_prev[0] = q[0] + disp_prev[0] + shift[0];
2285 pos_prev[1] = q[1] + disp_prev[1] + shift[1];
2286 pos_prev[2] = q[2] + disp_prev[2] + shift[2];
2287 pos_curr[0] = q[0] + disp_curr[0] + shift[0];
2288 pos_curr[1] = q[1] + disp_curr[1] + shift[1];
2289 pos_curr[2] = q[2] + disp_curr[2] + shift[2];
2290 }
2291
2292 /* Crossing logic:
2293 Define H_prev = chi_prev - r_prev and H_curr = chi_curr - r_curr.
2294 Crossing occurs if H_prev > 0 and H_curr <= 0. Estimate alpha by
2295 linear interpolation of H between the two endpoints. */
2296 double c0 = plc.center[0] * params.InterPartDist;
2297 double c1 = plc.center[1] * params.InterPartDist;
2298 double c2 = plc.center[2] * params.InterPartDist;
2299 double dxp0 = pos_prev[0] - c0, dyp0 = pos_prev[1] - c1, dzp0 = pos_prev[2] - c2;
2300 double dxp1 = pos_curr[0] - c0, dyp1 = pos_curr[1] - c1, dzp1 = pos_curr[2] - c2;
2301 double r_prev = sqrt(dxp0 * dxp0 + dyp0 * dyp0 + dzp0 * dzp0);
2302 double r_curr = sqrt(dxp1 * dxp1 + dyp1 * dyp1 + dzp1 * dzp1);
2303 double chi_prev = ComovingDistance(z_prev);
2304 double chi_curr = ComovingDistance(z_curr);
2305 double H_prev = chi_prev - r_prev;
2306 double H_curr = chi_curr - r_curr;
2307
2308 /* Early exit if no crossing according to crossing condition */
2309 if (!(H_prev > 0.0 && H_curr <= 0.0))
2310 {
2311 static int sanity_no_cross_prints = 0;
2312 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDIAG && sanity_no_cross_prints < 10)
2313 {
2314 printf("MASS_MAPS SANITY(no-entry): rep=%d z_prev=%.3f z_curr=%.3f H_prev=% .3e H_curr=% .3e r_prev=%.2f r_curr=%.2f chi_prev=%.2f chi_curr=%.2f\n",
2315 rep_id, z_prev, z_curr, H_prev, H_curr, r_prev, r_curr, chi_prev, chi_curr);
2316 sanity_no_cross_prints++;
2317 }
2318 return 0;
2319 }
2320
2321 double denom = H_prev - H_curr;
2322 if (fabs(denom) < MASS_MAPS_F_EPS)
2323 return 0; /* ambiguous, skip */
2324 double alpha = H_prev / denom; /* fraction from prev->curr to crossing */
2325 /* Clamp to [0,1] just in case */
2326 if (alpha < 0.0)
2327 alpha = 0.0;
2328 else if (alpha > 1.0)
2329 alpha = 1.0;
2330
2331 /* Optional sanity print for a few crossings */
2332 {
2333 static int sanity_cross_prints = 0;
2334 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDIAG && sanity_cross_prints < 10)
2335 {
2336 printf("MASS_MAPS SANITY(entry): rep=%d z_prev=%.3f z_curr=%.3f H_prev=% .3e H_curr=% .3e alpha=%.3f r_prev=%.2f r_curr=%.2f\n",
2337 rep_id, z_prev, z_curr, H_prev, H_curr, alpha, r_prev, r_curr);
2338 sanity_cross_prints++;
2339 }
2340 }
2341 if (alpha_out)
2342 *alpha_out = alpha;
2343 if (entry_pos)
2344 {
2345 entry_pos[0] = pos_prev[0] + alpha * (pos_curr[0] - pos_prev[0]);
2346 entry_pos[1] = pos_prev[1] + alpha * (pos_curr[1] - pos_prev[1]);
2347 entry_pos[2] = pos_prev[2] + alpha * (pos_curr[2] - pos_prev[2]);
2348 }
2349 return 1;

References _x_, _y_, _z_, plc_data::center, ComovingDistance(), grid_data::GSglobal, replication_data::i, internal, param_data::InterPartDist, replication_data::j, replication_data::k, MASS_MAPS_F_EPS, MyGrids, plc_data::Nreplications, params, plc, plc_data::repls, ThisTask, VDIAG, and internal_data::verbose_level.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ mass_maps_process_segment()

void mass_maps_process_segment ( int  segment_index,
double  z_segment,
int  is_first_segment 
)

mass_maps_process_segment

Orchestrate mass-map accumulation for one fragmentation segment using the "products" path (positions reconstructed from products and LPT displacements).

Parameters: segment_index - current segment index in [1..NMassSheets]; map index is s_map=segment_index-1 z_segment - redshift of the current segment (typically ScaleDep.z[segment_index]) is_first_segment - non-zero for the first segment; no crossings are computed and the function returns early

Behavior:

  • Lazy allocation: on first invocation, allocate HEALPix maps for all sheets if params.MassMapNSIDE>0 (RING ordering).
  • Optional filter refresh: if MASS_MAPS_FILTER_UNCOLLAPSED and SNAPSHOT are defined, call distribute_back() so products[].zacc and products[].group_ID reflect current memberships before filtering/diagnostics; prints coverage diagnostics at VDBG.
  • Select replication candidates whose shifted Lagrangian AABBs overlap the segment in comoving distance (radial prune only).
  • Compute segment chi bounds and precompute per-candidate replication shifts in physical units (true Mpc).
  • Optional Lagrangian culling: if MASS_MAPS_BLOCKS_* > 0, split the local tile into blocks; build a padded AABB (variable pad in true Mpc via MASS_MAPS_VARPAD_FACTOR) and cull by radial [dmin,dmax] vs [chi_min,chi_max] and a conservative angular bound against the PLC aperture; reuse cached prev/curr positions per block; optionally use per-thread LocalMap (MASS_MAPS_THREAD_ACCUM) to reduce atomic contention.
  • For each particle and passing replication: • Build previous and current Eulerian positions (mass_maps_compute_prev_curr_positions), apply replication shift. • Detect PLC boundary crossing inside the segment using mass_maps_crossing_from_shifted_positions with H=chi−r predicate. • Check angular aperture; optionally filter for uncollapsed-only (ZPLC>ZACC). • Accumulate one count into the HEALPix pixel of the entry position for sheet s_map.
  • Diagnostics: track per-rep and total entries; optional endpoint displacement vs buffer stats on the last segment; filter diagnostics and optional matching diagnostics.
  • MPI reductions: reduce per-rep counts and the segment map to rank 0, which writes the FITS file via mass_maps_write_segment_map(s_map) and prints map stats at VDBG.

Threading and MPI:

  • OpenMP over blocks (when enabled); per-pixel updates use atomics or thread-local maps merged once per block. All ranks compute local contributions; rank 0 performs I/O.

Units and conventions:

  • Positions and shifts are in true Mpc (InterPartDist units). ComovingDistance maps z→chi.
  • HEALPix uses RING ordering; aperture is in degrees. Map index s_map = segment_index-1.

Early exits:

  • Feature disabled or not initialized (NMassSheets<=0 or params.MassMapNSIDE<=0).
  • First segment (no previous state to form crossings): returns immediately.

Side effects:

  • Allocates global map storage on first use; persists across segments until freed by mass_maps_free_maps(). Updates global diagnostics used in FITS headers when filtering.

Definition at line 2409 of file write_mass_maps.c.

2410{
2411 /* Ensure ZACC values are available in products when filtering uncollapsed-only */
2412#if defined(MASS_MAPS_FILTER_UNCOLLAPSED)
2413#if defined(SNAPSHOT)
2414 {
2415 /* Do back-distribution at EACH segment, as fragmentation updates across segments
2416 can assign new halo memberships and zacc values. This ensures the filter and
2417 matching diagnostics see up-to-date products[].zacc and products[].group_ID. */
2418 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDBG)
2419 printf("[%s] MASS_MAPS(filter): distributing ZACC/group_ID to products for segment %d\n", fdate(), segment_index);
2420 int rc = distribute_back();
2421 if (rc && !ThisTask)
2422 {
2423 fprintf(stderr, "[%s] MASS_MAPS(filter) WARNING: distribute_back() failed; ZACC may be unset (=-1) and the filter will include all.\n", fdate());
2424 }
2425#ifdef MASS_MAPS_MATCH_DIAG
2426 /* After back-distribution, estimate coverage of group_ID and zacc on this task */
2427 unsigned long long gid_pos_local = 0ULL, zacc_set_local = 0ULL;
2428 unsigned long long gid_pos_global = 0ULL, zacc_set_global = 0ULL;
2429 int nx = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_x_];
2430 int ny = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_y_];
2431 int nz = (int)MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];
2432 for (int li = 0; li < nx; ++li)
2433 for (int lj = 0; lj < ny; ++lj)
2434 for (int lk = 0; lk < nz; ++lk)
2435 {
2436 unsigned int idx = COORD_TO_INDEX(li, lj, lk, MyGrids[0].GSlocal);
2437 if (products[idx].group_ID > FILAMENT)
2438 gid_pos_local++;
2439 if (products[idx].zacc > -0.5)
2440 zacc_set_local++;
2441 }
2442 MPI_Reduce(&gid_pos_local, &gid_pos_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
2443 MPI_Reduce(&zacc_set_local, &zacc_set_global, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
2444 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDBG)
2445 {
2446 unsigned long long total_local_size = (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] * (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] * (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_];
2447 double f_gid = (total_local_size > 0ULL) ? ((double)gid_pos_global / (double)total_local_size) : 0.0;
2448 double f_zacc = (total_local_size > 0ULL) ? ((double)zacc_set_global / (double)total_local_size) : 0.0;
2449 printf("[%s] MASS_MAPS(match): products coverage after back-distribution: frac(group_ID>FILAMENT)=%.3f frac(zacc_set)=%.3f\n",
2450 fdate(), f_gid, f_zacc);
2451 }
2452#endif
2453 }
2454#else
2455#error "MASS_MAPS_FILTER_UNCOLLAPSED requires SNAPSHOT"
2456#endif
2457#endif
2458 /* One-time HEALPix map allocator */
2459 {
2460 static int hpix_init_done = 0;
2461 if (!hpix_init_done)
2462 {
2463 if (params.MassMapNSIDE > 0)
2464 mass_maps_init_healpix_maps(params.MassMapNSIDE);
2465 hpix_init_done = 1;
2466 }
2467 }
2468 /* One-time units diagnostic to stdout (debug-level) */
2469 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDBG)
2470 {
2471 static int printed_units = 0;
2472 if (!printed_units)
2473 {
2474 double c0 = plc.center[0] * params.InterPartDist;
2475 double c1 = plc.center[1] * params.InterPartDist;
2476 double c2 = plc.center[2] * params.InterPartDist;
2477 double Bx = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] * params.InterPartDist;
2478 double By = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] * params.InterPartDist;
2479 double Bz = MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_] * params.InterPartDist;
2480 printf("[%s] MASS_MAPS units: PLC center (phys)=(%.9g, %.9g, %.9g) InterPartDist=%.9g BoxSize=(%.9g, %.9g, %.9g)\n",
2481 fdate(), c0, c1, c2, params.InterPartDist, Bx, By, Bz);
